kids.question: What does John say God is?
kids.option_a: God is light, and in him is no darkness at all
kids.option_b: God is a great king on a throne
kids.option_c: God is like the wind that blows
kids.explanation: 1 John 1:5 — "God is light, and in him is no darkness at all."

kids.question: What does the blood of Jesus do for us?
kids.explanation: 1 John 1:7 — "The blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from all sin."

kids.question: What should we do when we sin?
kids.explanation: 1 John 1:9 — "If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness."

teens.question: What does John say he and the apostles have seen, heard, and handled?
teens.option_a: The Word of life — that which was from the beginning
teens.option_b: A vision of angels
teens.option_c: The temple in Jerusalem
teens.explanation: 1 John 1:1 — "That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon, and our hands have handled, of the Word of life."

teens.question: Why is John writing about what he has witnessed?
teens.option_a: That his readers may have fellowship with them and with the Father and the Son
teens.option_b: To prove he is a real apostle
teens.option_c: To write a history book
teens.explanation: 1 John 1:3 — "That which we have seen and heard declare we unto you, that ye also may have fellowship with us: and truly our fellowship is with the Father, and with his Son Jesus Christ."

teens.question: What happens if we walk in the light as God is in the light?
teens.option_a: We have fellowship one with another, and the blood of Jesus cleanseth us from all sin
teens.option_b: We become perfect and never sin again
teens.option_c: We receive earthly rewards and riches
teens.option_d: We are protected from all suffering
teens.explanation: 1 John 1:7 — "But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship one with another, and the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from all sin."

teens.question: What does John say about claiming to have no sin?
teens.option_a: We deceive ourselves and the truth is not in us
teens.option_b: It means we have reached perfection
teens.option_c: It is the goal of every believer
teens.explanation: 1 John 1:8 — "If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us."

teens.question: What two things does God do when we confess our sins?
teens.option_a: He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness
teens.option_b: He punishes us and then forgives
teens.option_c: He forgives but remembers forever
teens.explanation: 1 John 1:9 — "If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness."

adults.question: What does John's opening phrase "That which was from the beginning" echo?
adults.option_a: The opening of John's Gospel — "In the beginning was the Word" — affirming Christ's eternal preexistence
adults.option_b: The creation account in Genesis 1
adults.option_c: The call of Abraham
adults.option_d: The giving of the law at Sinai
adults.explanation: 1 John 1:1 — "That which was from the beginning" parallels John 1:1, affirming the eternal nature of the Word of life.

adults.question: What does John mean by "the eternal life, which was with the Father, and was manifested unto us"?
adults.option_a: Christ himself is the eternal life who existed with the Father and became incarnate
adults.option_b: Eternal life is a concept taught by the prophets
adults.option_c: Eternal life began when believers first believed
adults.option_d: Eternal life is a future promise not yet realized
adults.explanation: 1 John 1:2 — "For the life was manifested, and we have seen it, and bear witness, and shew unto you that eternal life, which was with the Father, and was manifested unto us."

adults.question: Why does John write these things according to verse 4?
adults.option_a: That your joy may be full
adults.option_b: That you may fear God more
adults.option_c: That you may escape persecution
adults.option_d: That you may gain wisdom
adults.explanation: 1 John 1:4 — "And these things write we unto you, that your joy may be full."

adults.question: What does John say about claiming fellowship with God while walking in darkness?
adults.option_a: We lie and do not the truth
adults.option_b: We are still saved but immature
adults.option_c: We are being tested by God
adults.option_d: We are in a normal phase of growth
adults.explanation: 1 John 1:6 — "If we say that we have fellowship with him, and walk in darkness, we lie, and do not the truth."

adults.question: What does the statement "God is light, and in him is no darkness at all" teach about God's nature?
adults.option_a: God is absolutely holy, pure, and without any moral imperfection whatsoever
adults.option_b: God is a physical source of visible light
adults.option_c: God sometimes permits darkness for good purposes
adults.option_d: God's light only shines on the righteous
adults.explanation: 1 John 1:5 — "God is light, and in him is no darkness at all." The emphatic double negative in Greek stresses absolute moral purity.

adults.question: What does "faithful and just" in verse 9 reveal about forgiveness?
adults.option_a: God's forgiveness is rooted in his faithfulness to his covenant and the justice satisfied by Christ's atonement
adults.option_b: God forgives because he overlooks sin
adults.option_c: God forgives arbitrarily based on feelings
adults.option_d: God forgives only those who earn it through works
adults.explanation: 1 John 1:9 — "He is faithful and just to forgive us." God's forgiveness flows from his covenant faithfulness and the just satisfaction of Christ's sacrifice.

adults.question: What does John say we make God if we say we have not sinned?
adults.option_a: We make him a liar, and his word is not in us
adults.option_b: We make him proud of us
adults.option_c: We make him our judge
adults.option_d: We make him irrelevant
adults.explanation: 1 John 1:10 — "If we say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us."

adults.question: What four sensory verbs does John use in verse 1 to establish apostolic witness?
adults.option_a: Heard, seen, looked upon, and handled — emphasizing the physical reality of Christ's incarnation
adults.option_b: Read, studied, memorized, and taught
adults.option_c: Dreamed, envisioned, prophesied, and imagined
adults.option_d: Believed, hoped, trusted, and obeyed
adults.explanation: 1 John 1:1 — "Which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon, and our hands have handled." These verbs counter Gnostic denial of Christ's physical body.

kids.question: If we sin, who speaks to the Father for us?
kids.option_a: Jesus Christ the righteous, our advocate
kids.explanation: 1 John 2:1 — "If any man sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous."

kids.question: What does John say about loving your brother?
kids.option_a: He that loveth his brother abideth in the light
kids.option_c: You only need to love your close friends
kids.explanation: 1 John 2:10 — "He that loveth his brother abideth in the light, and there is none occasion of stumbling in him."

kids.question: What does John say we should NOT love?
kids.option_a: The world, neither the things that are in the world
kids.explanation: 1 John 2:15 — "Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world."

teens.question: How do we know that we know Christ?
teens.option_a: If we keep his commandments
teens.option_b: If we attend church every week
teens.option_c: If we feel happy all the time
teens.option_d: If we have visions and dreams
teens.explanation: 1 John 2:3 — "And hereby we do know that we know him, if we keep his commandments."

teens.question: What three things of the world does John warn against?
teens.option_a: The lust of the flesh, the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life
teens.option_b: Money, fame, and power
teens.option_c: Food, clothing, and shelter
teens.explanation: 1 John 2:16 — "For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world."

teens.question: What does John say about the world and its lusts?
teens.option_a: The world passeth away, and the lust thereof, but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever
teens.option_b: The world will last forever
teens.option_c: The world's pleasures are from God
teens.option_d: The lusts of the world are harmless
teens.explanation: 1 John 2:17 — "And the world passeth away, and the lust thereof: but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever."

teens.question: What does John call those who deny that Jesus is the Christ?
teens.option_a: Antichrist — he that denieth the Father and the Son
teens.option_b: A false prophet only
teens.option_c: A confused believer
teens.explanation: 1 John 2:22 — "Who is a liar but he that denieth that Jesus is the Christ? He is antichrist, that denieth the Father and the Son."

teens.question: What anointing have believers received?
teens.option_a: An unction (anointing) from the Holy One, and ye know all things
teens.option_b: An anointing with oil by the elders
teens.option_c: A special blessing from the apostles
teens.option_d: A physical mark on their foreheads
teens.explanation: 1 John 2:20 — "But ye have an unction from the Holy One, and ye know all things."

adults.question: What does John call Jesus in his role before the Father?
adults.option_a: An advocate with the Father — Jesus Christ the righteous
adults.option_b: A judge who condemns the guilty
adults.option_c: A prophet who reveals the future
adults.option_d: A priest who offers sacrifices
adults.explanation: 1 John 2:1 — "We have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous." The Greek "parakletos" means one called alongside to help.

adults.question: What is the scope of Christ's propitiation according to verse 2?
adults.option_a: Not for our sins only, but also for the sins of the whole world
adults.option_b: Only for the sins of the apostles
adults.option_c: Only for the sins of Israel
adults.option_d: Only for sins committed before conversion
adults.explanation: 1 John 2:2 — "And he is the propitiation for our sins: and not for ours only, but also for the sins of the whole world."

adults.question: What does John say about one who claims to know God but does not keep his commandments?
adults.option_a: He is a liar, and the truth is not in him
adults.option_b: He is a new believer who needs patience
adults.option_c: He is struggling but still faithful
adults.option_d: He is under grace and free from the law
adults.explanation: 1 John 2:4 — "He that saith, I know him, and keepeth not his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in him."

adults.question: What does John say about the new commandment he writes?
adults.option_a: It is not new but old, yet also new — because the darkness is past and the true light now shineth
adults.option_b: It completely replaces the Old Testament law
adults.option_c: It was never taught before
adults.option_d: It applies only to church leaders
adults.explanation: 1 John 2:7-8 — "Brethren, I write no new commandment unto you, but an old commandment... Again, a new commandment I write unto you... because the darkness is past, and the true light now shineth."

adults.question: What does John say about those who went out from the believers?
adults.option_a: They went out from us because they were not of us — if they had been of us, they would have continued with us
adults.option_b: They left to start better churches
adults.option_c: They were sent on missionary journeys
adults.option_d: They were promoted to higher positions
adults.explanation: 1 John 2:19 — "They went out from us, but they were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would no doubt have continued with us."

adults.question: Who is the liar according to verse 22?
adults.option_a: He that denieth that Jesus is the Christ — he is antichrist, that denieth the Father and the Son
adults.option_b: He who tells falsehoods to neighbours
adults.option_c: He who breaks business contracts
adults.option_d: He who exaggerates his accomplishments
adults.explanation: 1 John 2:22 — "Who is a liar but he that denieth that Jesus is the Christ? He is antichrist, that denieth the Father and the Son."

adults.question: What does John say about the anointing believers received?
adults.option_a: The anointing which ye have received of him abideth in you, and ye need not that any man teach you
adults.option_b: It must be renewed every year by church elders
adults.option_c: It was a one-time physical ceremony
adults.option_d: It only applies to ordained ministers
adults.explanation: 1 John 2:27 — "But the anointing which ye have received of him abideth in you, and ye need not that any man teach you: but as the same anointing teacheth you of all things."

adults.question: What three groups does John address in verses 12-14 and what characterizes each?
adults.option_a: Little children (sins forgiven, know the Father), young men (overcome the wicked one, strong, word abideth), fathers (known him from the beginning)
adults.option_b: Infants, teenagers, and senior citizens
adults.option_c: New converts, deacons, and elders
adults.option_d: Students, teachers, and prophets
adults.explanation: 1 John 2:12-14 — John addresses "little children" (sins forgiven), "fathers" (known him from the beginning), and "young men" (overcome the wicked one, strong, word of God abideth in you).

kids.question: What does God call us?
kids.explanation: 1 John 3:1 — "Behold, what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called the sons of God."

kids.question: What does John say we should do for each other?
kids.explanation: 1 John 3:11 — "For this is the message that ye heard from the beginning, that we should love one another."

kids.question: How should we love — just with words, or with actions too?
kids.option_a: Not in word only, but in deed and in truth
kids.explanation: 1 John 3:18 — "My little children, let us not love in word, neither in tongue; but in deed and in truth."

teens.question: What will we be like when Christ appears?
teens.option_a: We shall be like him, for we shall see him as he is
teens.option_b: We will become invisible spirits
teens.option_c: We will remain exactly as we are now
teens.explanation: 1 John 3:2 — "We know that, when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is."

teens.question: What is sin according to 1 John 3:4?
teens.option_a: Sin is the transgression of the law
teens.option_b: Sin is only harming other people
teens.option_c: Sin is whatever society disapproves of
teens.option_d: Sin is only what you do on purpose
teens.explanation: 1 John 3:4 — "Whosoever committeth sin transgresseth also the law: for sin is the transgression of the law."

teens.question: Why did Cain slay his brother Abel?
teens.option_a: Because his own works were evil, and his brother's righteous
teens.option_b: Because Abel stole from him
teens.option_c: Because their father told him to
teens.explanation: 1 John 3:12 — "Not as Cain, who was of that wicked one, and slew his brother. And wherefore slew he him? Because his own works were evil, and his brother's righteous."

teens.question: How do we know we have passed from death to life?
teens.option_a: Because we love the brethren
teens.option_b: Because we attend church regularly
teens.option_c: Because we have been baptized
teens.explanation: 1 John 3:14 — "We know that we have passed from death unto life, because we love the brethren."

teens.question: How did we come to know love?
teens.option_a: Because Christ laid down his life for us, and we ought to lay down our lives for the brethren
teens.option_b: Through reading books about love
teens.option_c: Through romantic relationships
teens.explanation: 1 John 3:16 — "Hereby perceive we the love of God, because he laid down his life for us: and we ought to lay down our lives for the brethren."

adults.question: What manner of love has the Father bestowed upon believers?
adults.option_a: That we should be called the sons of God — and the reason the world knoweth us not is because it knew him not
adults.option_b: That we should become wealthy and powerful
adults.option_c: That we should rule over the nations
adults.option_d: That we should be free from all suffering
adults.explanation: 1 John 3:1 — "Behold, what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called the sons of God: therefore the world knoweth us not, because it knew him not."

adults.question: What does John say about everyone who has the hope of Christ's appearing?
adults.option_a: Every man that hath this hope in him purifieth himself, even as he is pure
adults.option_b: He may live however he pleases
adults.option_c: He need not worry about holiness
adults.option_d: He is already perfected
adults.explanation: 1 John 3:3 — "And every man that hath this hope in him purifieth himself, even as he is pure."

adults.question: Why was the Son of God manifested according to verse 8?
adults.option_a: That he might destroy the works of the devil
adults.option_b: That he might establish an earthly kingdom
adults.option_c: That he might write new scriptures
adults.option_d: That he might train twelve apostles
adults.explanation: 1 John 3:8 — "For this purpose the Son of God was manifested, that he might destroy the works of the devil."

adults.question: What does John say about whosoever is born of God in verse 9?
adults.option_a: He doth not commit sin, for his seed remaineth in him, and he cannot sin because he is born of God
adults.option_b: He never makes any mistakes at all
adults.option_c: He immediately becomes sinlessly perfect
adults.option_d: He is free to sin without consequences
adults.explanation: 1 John 3:9 — "Whosoever is born of God doth not commit sin; for his seed remaineth in him: and he cannot sin, because he is born of God." This describes the habitual practice of sin being incompatible with the new birth.

adults.question: What does John say about the one who hates his brother?
adults.option_a: He is a murderer, and no murderer hath eternal life abiding in him
adults.option_b: He is just having a bad day
adults.option_c: He needs counselling but is still saved
adults.option_d: He is exercising righteous judgment
adults.explanation: 1 John 3:15 — "Whosoever hateth his brother is a murderer: and ye know that no murderer hath eternal life abiding in him."

adults.question: How does John define the failure to love practically?
adults.option_a: Whoso hath this world's good and seeth his brother have need and shutteth up his bowels of compassion — how dwelleth the love of God in him?
adults.option_b: Failing to pray for someone daily
adults.option_c: Not attending every church meeting
adults.option_d: Not giving to the temple treasury
adults.explanation: 1 John 3:17 — "But whoso hath this world's good, and seeth his brother have need, and shutteth up his bowels of compassion from him, how dwelleth the love of God in him?"

adults.question: How can believers have confidence before God?
adults.option_a: If our heart condemn us not, then have we confidence toward God, and whatsoever we ask we receive because we keep his commandments
adults.option_b: Through elaborate rituals and sacrifices
adults.option_c: Through the intercession of church leaders
adults.option_d: Through accumulating good works
adults.explanation: 1 John 3:21-22 — "If our heart condemn us not, then have we confidence toward God. And whatsoever we ask, we receive of him, because we keep his commandments."

adults.question: What is God's commandment according to verse 23?
adults.option_a: That we should believe on the name of his Son Jesus Christ, and love one another
adults.option_b: That we keep the Sabbath and the feasts
adults.option_c: That we tithe and give offerings
adults.option_d: That we fast twice a week
adults.explanation: 1 John 3:23 — "And this is his commandment, That we should believe on the name of his Son Jesus Christ, and love one another, as he gave us commandment."

kids.question: What does John say God is?
kids.explanation: 1 John 4:8 — "He that loveth not knoweth not God; for God is love."

kids.question: Why did God send his Son into the world?
kids.option_a: That we might live through him
kids.option_b: So the world would be destroyed
kids.explanation: 1 John 4:9 — "In this was manifested the love of God toward us, because that God sent his only begotten Son into the world, that we might live through him."

kids.question: Why do we love God?
kids.explanation: 1 John 4:19 — "We love him, because he first loved us."

teens.question: What should believers do with every spirit?
teens.option_a: Try the spirits whether they are of God, because many false prophets are gone out into the world
teens.option_b: Accept all spirits as from God
teens.option_c: Ignore all spiritual matters
teens.explanation: 1 John 4:1 — "Beloved, believe not every spirit, but try the spirits whether they are of God: because many false prophets are gone out into the world."

teens.question: How can you know the Spirit of God?
teens.option_a: Every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is of God
teens.option_b: Every spirit that performs miracles
teens.option_c: Every spirit that makes you feel good
teens.option_d: Every spirit that predicts the future
teens.explanation: 1 John 4:2 — "Hereby know ye the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is of God."

teens.question: Who is greater — the Spirit in believers or the spirit in the world?
teens.option_a: Greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world
teens.option_b: The spirit in the world is stronger
teens.option_c: They are equal in power
teens.explanation: 1 John 4:4 — "Ye are of God, little children, and have overcome them: because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world."

teens.question: What does perfect love do to fear?
teens.option_a: Perfect love casteth out fear
teens.option_b: Perfect love increases fear of God
teens.option_c: Perfect love ignores fear
teens.option_d: Perfect love creates a new kind of fear
teens.explanation: 1 John 4:18 — "There is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear: because fear hath torment."

teens.question: What does John say about someone who claims to love God but hates his brother?
teens.option_a: He is a liar, for he that loveth not his brother whom he hath seen cannot love God whom he hath not seen
teens.option_b: He is just struggling with a difficult relationship
teens.option_c: He can still love God even while hating his brother
teens.option_d: He needs to work harder at both
teens.explanation: 1 John 4:20 — "If a man say, I love God, and hateth his brother, he is a liar: for he that loveth not his brother whom he hath seen, how can he love God whom he hath not seen?"

adults.question: What is the spirit of antichrist according to verse 3?
adults.option_a: Every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is not of God — this is that spirit of antichrist
adults.option_b: Any spirit that opposes the Roman government
adults.option_c: Any spirit that disagrees with the apostles on minor points
adults.option_d: Any spirit that refuses to join a local church
adults.explanation: 1 John 4:3 — "And every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is not of God: and this is that spirit of antichrist."

adults.question: What does "herein is love" mean in verse 10?
adults.option_a: Not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins
adults.option_b: That we loved God first and he responded
adults.option_c: That we earned God's love through obedience
adults.option_d: That love is a mutual exchange between equals
adults.explanation: 1 John 4:10 — "Herein is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins."

adults.question: What does John say about seeing God in verse 12?
adults.option_a: No man hath seen God at any time; if we love one another, God dwelleth in us, and his love is perfected in us
adults.option_b: Many prophets have seen God face to face
adults.option_c: Only Moses and Elijah have seen God
adults.option_d: God is visible to anyone with faith
adults.explanation: 1 John 4:12 — "No man hath seen God at any time. If we love one another, God dwelleth in us, and his love is perfected in us."

adults.question: What confession does John say leads to God dwelling in a person?
adults.option_a: Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him, and he in God
adults.option_b: Whoever confesses belief in one God
adults.option_c: Whoever confesses all sins publicly
adults.option_d: Whoever confesses allegiance to the church
adults.explanation: 1 John 4:15 — "Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him, and he in God."

adults.question: What does John say about God's love being perfected in believers?
adults.option_a: Herein is our love made perfect, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment: because as he is, so are we in this world
adults.option_b: God's love makes us sinlessly perfect in this life
adults.option_c: God's love removes the day of judgment entirely
adults.option_d: God's love is only perfected in heaven
adults.explanation: 1 John 4:17 — "Herein is our love made perfect, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment: because as he is, so are we in this world."

adults.question: Why does fear have torment according to verse 18?
adults.option_a: Because fear hath torment, and he that feareth is not made perfect in love
adults.option_b: Because fear is a sign of unbelief
adults.option_c: Because fear leads to physical illness
adults.option_d: Because fear causes poverty
adults.explanation: 1 John 4:18 — "There is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear: because fear hath torment. He that feareth is not made perfect in love."

adults.question: How do we know that we dwell in God and he in us?
adults.option_a: Because he hath given us of his Spirit
adults.option_b: Because we feel emotions during worship
adults.option_c: Because we have been baptized in water
adults.option_d: Because we belong to a church
adults.explanation: 1 John 4:13 — "Hereby know we that we dwell in him, and he in us, because he hath given us of his Spirit."

adults.question: What commandment has he who loves God also received?
adults.option_a: That he who loveth God love his brother also
adults.option_b: That he who loves God must fast twice weekly
adults.option_c: That he who loves God must give all possessions away
adults.option_d: That he who loves God must preach in public
adults.explanation: 1 John 4:21 — "And this commandment have we from him, That he who loveth God love his brother also."

kids.question: What overcomes the world?
kids.explanation: 1 John 5:4 — "And this is the victory that overcometh the world, even our faith."

kids.question: What has God given to us through his Son?
kids.explanation: 1 John 5:11 — "And this is the record, that God hath given to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son."

kids.question: Why did John write this letter?
kids.option_a: That ye may know that ye have eternal life
kids.explanation: 1 John 5:13 — "These things have I written unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal life."

teens.question: What is the victory that overcometh the world?
teens.option_a: Even our faith
teens.option_b: Our military might
teens.option_c: Our political power
teens.explanation: 1 John 5:4 — "And this is the victory that overcometh the world, even our faith."

teens.question: What three things bear witness in the earth?
teens.option_a: The Spirit, the water, and the blood — and these three agree in one
teens.option_b: The sun, the moon, and the stars
teens.option_c: The prophets, the priests, and the kings
teens.option_d: The Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost
teens.explanation: 1 John 5:8 — "And there are three that bear witness in earth, the Spirit, and the water, and the blood: and these three agree in one."

teens.question: What confidence do believers have according to verse 14?
teens.option_a: If we ask any thing according to his will, he heareth us
teens.option_b: God will give us whatever we want
teens.option_c: God only hears prayers on certain days
teens.option_d: God hears only the prayers of the elders
teens.explanation: 1 John 5:14 — "And this is the confidence that we have in him, that, if we ask any thing according to his will, he heareth us."

teens.question: What does John say about sin unto death?
teens.option_a: There is a sin unto death; I do not say that he shall pray for it
teens.option_b: Every sin leads to physical death
teens.option_c: There is no sin that leads to death
teens.option_d: Only murder is a sin unto death
teens.explanation: 1 John 5:16 — "There is a sin unto death: I do not say that he shall pray for it."

teens.question: What does John tell believers to keep themselves from?
teens.option_a: Idols
teens.option_b: All food offered to idols
teens.option_c: All Gentile friends
teens.explanation: 1 John 5:21 — "Little children, keep yourselves from idols. Amen."

adults.question: Who is it that overcometh the world according to verse 5?
adults.option_a: He that believeth that Jesus is the Son of God
adults.option_b: He that keeps all the commandments perfectly
adults.option_c: He that performs the most miracles
adults.option_d: He that has the most Bible knowledge
adults.explanation: 1 John 5:5 — "Who is he that overcometh the world, but he that believeth that Jesus is the Son of God?"

adults.question: What does John say about Jesus coming by water and blood?
adults.option_a: This is he that came by water and blood, even Jesus Christ; not by water only, but by water and blood
adults.option_b: Christ came only by water baptism
adults.option_c: Christ came only by the blood of the covenant
adults.option_d: Christ came by neither water nor blood
adults.explanation: 1 John 5:6 — "This is he that came by water and blood, even Jesus Christ; not by water only, but by water and blood. And it is the Spirit that beareth witness, because the Spirit is truth."

adults.question: What does John say about the witness of God versus the witness of men?
adults.option_a: If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater — for this is the witness of God which he hath testified of his Son
adults.option_b: The witness of men is more reliable
adults.option_c: Both witnesses are equally valid
adults.option_d: God does not give witness to men
adults.explanation: 1 John 5:9 — "If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater: for this is the witness of God which he hath testified of his Son."

adults.question: What does John say about the one who does not believe God?
adults.option_a: He hath made him a liar, because he believeth not the record that God gave of his Son
adults.option_b: He simply needs more time to understand
adults.option_c: He is being cautious and wise
adults.option_d: He has not heard the truth yet
adults.explanation: 1 John 5:10 — "He that believeth not God hath made him a liar; because he believeth not the record that God gave of his Son."

adults.question: What is the explicit purpose of 1 John according to verse 13?
adults.option_a: That ye may know that ye have eternal life, and that ye may believe on the name of the Son of God
adults.option_b: To provide a systematic theology
adults.option_c: To give a history of the early church
adults.option_d: To prophesy about future events
adults.explanation: 1 John 5:13 — "These things have I written unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal life."

adults.question: What does John say about whosoever is born of God and sin?
adults.option_a: He that is born of God keepeth himself, and that wicked one toucheth him not
adults.option_b: He is immune to all forms of temptation
adults.option_c: He can sin freely because grace abounds
adults.option_d: He must rely on church leaders for protection
adults.explanation: 1 John 5:18 — "We know that whosoever is born of God sinneth not; but he that is begotten of God keepeth himself, and that wicked one toucheth him not."

adults.question: What does John say about the whole world in verse 19?
adults.option_a: The whole world lieth in wickedness (or, in the wicked one)
adults.option_b: The whole world is gradually getting better
adults.option_c: The whole world belongs to believers
adults.option_d: The whole world has been redeemed
adults.explanation: 1 John 5:19 — "And we know that we are of God, and the whole world lieth in wickedness."

adults.question: What Christological affirmation does John make in verse 20?
adults.option_a: This is the true God, and eternal life — affirming that Jesus Christ is the true God
adults.option_b: Jesus is only a great teacher
adults.option_c: Jesus is the highest created being
adults.option_d: Jesus is a prophet like Moses
adults.explanation: 1 John 5:20 — "And we know that the Son of God is come, and hath given us an understanding, that we may know him that is true, and we are in him that is true, even in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God, and eternal life."
